input.background: To examine the relationship between male-pattern baldness and myocardial infarction, investigators studied 655 men admitted to a hospital for a first nonfatal myocardial infarction (cases) and 772 admitted to the same hospitals with noncardiac diagnoses (controls). This is a case-control sample because the investigator selected fixed numbers of subjects based on the status of the response variable (myocardial infarction).
input.question: The extent of baldness was assessed in subjects using several methods.

Number_of_cases.description: The number of men admitted to a hospital for a first nonfatal myocardial infarction.

Number_of_controls.description: The number of men admitted to the same hospitals with noncardiac diagnoses.

Baldness_extent.description: The extent of baldness in subjects, assessed using several methods.

Myocardial_infarction_status.description: The status of myocardial infarction in subjects, either as cases or controls.
